authorityHierarchy: Target governance and AGENTS.md restrictions define allowed architecture and write scope.
pgnViewer-new is canonical runtime and visual-value authority for values and interactions.
chess-pgnviewer docs are governance authority for token/theme/persistence/accessibility/security rules; React runtime code is not target architecture.
pgnViewer is secondary capability evidence only when canonical evidence is absent or ambiguous; not needed for this F3A conclusion.

missingThemeMechanisms: No data-theme marker in index.html or Vue bootstrap
No color-scheme declaration in target runtime HTML/CSS except canonical docs
No Naive UI provider or theme override integration

tokenNamespaceDecision.decision: Keep canonical values in the implemented global CSS custom-property registry; canonical TS objects and feature-local raw values are not token authority.
compatibilityAliasesAllowed: --panel/--input/--fg/--muted legacy aliases only inside token authority
tokenNamespaceDecision.duplicateNamespaceDisposition: Canonical --cg-light/--cg-dark and --panel/--input/--fg/--muted are aliases only; feature code must migrate to semantic tokens.

themeModeDecision.canonicalEvidence: pgnViewer-new supports light/dark persisted as T:colorMode; target governance requires light/dark/system.
themeModeDecision.targetDecision: Persist user intent as themeMode light|dark|system; resolve applied mode to light|dark; listen to prefers-color-scheme only when themeMode is system.
themeModeDecision.persistence: src/bootstrap/preferences/themePreference.ts owns the current non-sensitive themeMode bootstrap record. Any later Dexie preference expansion requires an approved persistence contract; canonical-source storage is evidence only.

markers: html[data-display-theme="standard|display"] only when display route is active
rootThemeMarkerDecision.startup: The implemented prepaint bootstrap is minimal, same-origin, non-secret, mirrors the bootstrap preference contract, and updates color-scheme and meta[name=theme-color] before Vue mount.

missingThemeMechanisms: No data-theme marker in index.html or Vue bootstrap
No color-scheme declaration in target runtime HTML/CSS except canonical docs
No Naive UI provider or theme override integration

canonicalTokenInventory.securityImpact: No secret-bearing value; visual token only.

canonicalRuntimeThemeInventory.colorModeOwner: src/stores/themeStore.ts useThemeStore, localStorage keys T:colorMode and T:boardStyle, apply() writes html data-theme/data-board-style and --cg-* inline vars
canonicalRuntimeThemeInventory.prepaint: index.html lines 8-16 reads T:colorMode/T:boardStyle and writes data-theme/data-boardStyle before Vue mount
canonicalRuntimeThemeInventory.provider: src/app/App.vue watches theme store immediately and computes Naive light/dark theme and overrides
canonicalRuntimeThemeInventory.limitationForTarget: Canonical localStorage and TS raw palette storage conflict with target governance; values are canonical, ownership pattern is not.

naiveUiCanonicalInventory.targetDecision: Install Naive only behind project-owned Vue provider/adapter boundaries. Theme overrides must be derived from token names or a generated token manifest, not duplicated literals in component files.
missingInTarget: No direct Naive import in target src

canonical: BoardView motionDuration() checks prefers-reduced-motion before GSAP durations
BoardView uses GSAP durations 0.12/0.14/0.15/0.18/0.2/0.22 and eases power1.out/power3.out
PgnPanel and context menu use GSAP hover/active/list transitions
targetCurrent: src/styles/base.css global prefers-reduced-motion sets animation/transition duration 0.01ms
motionInventory.targetDecision: Add motion tokens in implementation slice, preserve canonical durations/eases as tokenized values, and keep runtime motionDuration guard for GSAP paths.

canonical: Global type and controls use clamp()
BoardView uses container query units cqw/cqh for coordinate gutters and board frame
Teaching workspace/panels use fixed viewport shell, local scroll, panel widths, right-panel stack, and mobile breakpoints around 760/900px in audited components
governance: docs/ui/RESPONSIVE_SCREEN_SPEC.md defines 640/1024/1440/1920 breakpoint tokens and env(safe-area-inset-*) handling
docs/ui/LAYOUT_SYSTEM_SPEC.md requires no body scroll and one scroll owner per area
targetCurrent: html/body/#app height 100% and body overflow hidden
No safe-area tokens, splitters, panel geometry, breakpoint values, or display overrides implemented
responsiveGeometryInventory.targetDecision: Tokenize breakpoint, safe-area, panel, splitter, toolbar, board, dialog, overlay, and scroll-surface geometry before feature migration.

boardTokenInventory.source: src/stores/themeStore.ts BOARD_STYLES and src/features/board/BoardView.vue SVG token consumers
boardTokenInventory.targetDecision: Board tokens stay independent from UI light/dark and are selected by board theme marker/contextual token block.

annotationTokenInventory.targetDecision: Preserve YCDW color compatibility but expose canonical colors/widths/opacities as tokens/domain constants with token ownership.

currentGaps: No high-contrast theme decision beyond canonical contrast board theme
No target board a11y runtime yet
accessibilityTokenInventory.validationRequirement: Future implementation must verify contrast, focus visibility, keyboard board path, and reduced motion in real browser for affected route.

f3aTargetConflictsAtInventory: Target token file has many placeholder values; canonical runtime values are not yet implemented.
Target docs require light/dark/system but canonical runtime only has light/dark; system mode must be target-owned extension.
Canonical runtime uses localStorage for appearance; target governance requires Dexie preference ownership and safe prepaint restoration.
Canonical Naive theme overrides duplicate raw values in TS; target must map through token authority/provider.
Target token names omit some canonical/state roles such as --accent, --hover, --active-bg, --ring-accent, --cg-coord-*, --cg-overlay-*, --cg-hint-*.
duplicateOrParallelNamespaces: Target --danger versus docs sometimes use error naming
Canonical Naive overrides as parallel raw-value theme namespace

targetManualFindings: Authorized token authority contains system colors Canvas/CanvasText/GrayText/Highlight, 1rem, 0ms, and global reduced-motion 0.01ms.
No raw runtime colors found outside accepted token authority by scanner.
canonicalRawValueInputs: feature component styles contain raw geometry/motion values to tokenize during migration

f3aUnresolvedEvidenceAtInventory: Canonical source does not provide system theme behavior; target must design system mode from governance requirements.
Canonical source does not provide meta theme-color behavior; target must derive values from --bg per mode during F3 implementation.
Canonical source does not provide a complete icon token namespace; derive from text/icon governance in target docs.
Canonical source contains feature-local raw geometry values; F3B/F3C must decide which become global tokens versus component-local constants in approved token authority.
Secondary pgnViewer evidence was not needed because pgnViewer-new and active governance were sufficient for F3A.

implementationSlices.scope: Implemented the global semantic token registry from the verified canonical inventory.
Implemented light/dark/system resolution, no-flash pre-Vue bootstrap, HTML markers, color-scheme, meta theme-color, preference fallback, system listeners, and cross-tab synchronization.
Implemented the project-owned Naive UI provider and token-derived theme overrides without duplicating raw values.

validationRequirements: Current implementation must preserve all 94 blocking governance rules.
Do not create automated tests or test scripts.
Run check:tokens first for token edits, then typecheck and build.
Visible UI/theme changes require one narrow real-browser runtime validation with nonblank DOM, no Vite overlay, no console-breaking errors, and an interaction/theme state change when applicable.
No package manager mutation and no dependency changes in F3 token/theme implementation unless separately approved.
